transcript.clean_text: with just over 10 weeks until the start of the first grand tour of the season details of the 2021 juror natalia have just been unveiled so we're going to take a first look at that route and let you know which stages in particular you really do not want to miss so the race will run from the 8th of may through to the 30th and cover the normal 21 stages of a grand tour those 21 stages include two individual time trials and what looks to be about five summit finishes in total it's 3 450 kilometers which start in torino and finish in milan now just before we get on to the nitty gritty of the race we wanted to let you know that you can watch the entire race live and on demand totally ad free on gcn plus and that's in all territories excluding new zealand and latin america and that means that yes even those of you who live in the usa or canada who've missed out on races in the past can now join us in watching the entire grand tour plus all the other rcs races like santa roma strada bianca il lombardia and many more on gcn plus for the very first time and we are incredibly excited about that don't forget also that you've got until the 28th of february to get the entire gsm plus package at 50 off so that's a whole year of live and on-demand bike racing our weekly world recycling show and all of our incredible epic original documentaries for a whole year for 20 quid 20 euros or 25 if you sign up before the end of february anyway that was my very best sales pitch out of the way so let's take a look at the route of what is my favorite grand tour of the year so the race will kick off on saturday the 8th of may in torino with a very short nine kilometer individual time trial now i don't expect the race will be one on this day of course but it will give us a very early indication of who is on form such a short time trial like that leaves little margin for error and any mechanicals or slip ups could lead to some early time losses for our general classification favorites now if he takes the start there in torino which appears to be the case then i think we can all agree that ineos grenadier's top ganner will probably win the stage and the first pink jersey of the race over the next few stages the route winds south before we get what looks like the first uphill finish that is a ascali piccino on stage six the appending climate of san giacomo is just over 16 kilometers in length and an average gradient of around six percent this never seems to exceed ten percent or so at the steepest point so it's not exactly a tough climb but it should weed out any general classification hopefuls who haven't got their preparation quite right and also show us who's brought their a game to the race before the end of the first week stage seven could be another one to watch as despite being 178 kilometers of flat racing a large proportion of this takes place on italy's eastern coastline between non telesco and tomolo which is a recipe for crosswinds and echelons which is a rarity in italian cycling but you just never know your luck as a viewer do you so it's an easy stage on paper that the race organizers rcs sport have only classified as two stars difficulty but one that weather dependent could have a very significant impact on the overall general classification the race after that begins to head north again with uphill finishes on stages eight and nine before a relatively benign looking day for the sprinters on stage 10 from la cuella to fallino before the first rest day comes on tuesday the 18th of may so that means that they've got 10 days of flat out racing before they get to briefly put their feet up for 24 hours or so racing then resumes the following day with stage 11 and it's definitely another one to watch not least because it's been coined as the brunello de montalcino wine stage kilometers of rolling tuscan road through the vineyards is prime puncher territory now this is going to be a short sharp stage but i would imagine it will be ridden like a one day classic with punctures and breakaway specialists putting everything on the line to try and take the spoils now on days like that one it is very likely that we'll see a general classification right or two be caught out and end up losing a chunk of time so we are expecting to see quite a nervous bunch after that initial rest day the route will then continue north through tuscany with a couple of long stages around about 200 kilometers in length and that's before we get to the first of the big and i mean big big mountain stages of the race the infamous montezunklan that is going to host the finish of stage 15. now this is a five-star stage and after two weeks of racing it's going to give us a really good indication as to who will be on the final podium come milan now the zonk line itself is truly brutal so it's 13 and a half kilometers long with an average gradient of nine percent but the maximum is 23 ouch i mean it's so steep that even the top climbers in the race are going to be swapping their normal gearing to compact to help ease with the suffering but this is also a day where my thoughts and prayers are going to be firmly with the riders in the gruppetto good luck guys now the last time that this climb appeared in the george italia was back in 2018 three years ago the stage itself won by chris froome writing at the time for team sky who then against all odds went on to win the race overall just one day's grace with a short flat transitional stage for another truly brutal mountain test comes on stage 16. that one includes four savage categorized climbs lagrossa setter passive vedaya passo por doy and a passo zhao before they descend to the finish in cortino so with the rest day following that this is going to be a very decisive stage in the race and would definitely be one to mark on your calendar the final rest day comes on may the 25th and that gives way to the race's roaring crescendo with three mountain stages two summit finishes and the race's longest day and the final individual time trial into milan so stage 17 from kalatzai to sega de alla covers 193 kilometers with an ascent of the paso de san valentino on the way to the summit finish of the sega de alla not a particularly well-known climb that one is it but it was used before at the giora del trentino where none other than vincenzo nieble took the win it's an 11 kilometer test with a gradient of just over nine percent on average and i think this has got the potential to be a very explosive climb and depending on how tight the general classification race is at that point it could be where the mallea rosa changes hands the 228 kilometer stage 18 the following day marks the longest of the entire race and hopefully this will be raised in its entirety this year as opposed to last and in stages 19 and 20 are both hard mountain days to finish off the road stages for this edition of the race and at 178 and 164 kilometers respectively i'd expect them to be raced pretty aggressively right from the gun we've got summit finish on stage 19 to alfa di mera and an ascent of both the paso de san bernardino and the paso de las pluga on stage 20 which briefly crosses the border into switzerland so if you have the time i would suggest trying to catch both of those stages in their entirety at least all the live coverage there's something about the final few days of racing at the dura italia that is just really really special and it's always full of surprises now in somewhat traditional fashion the race then concludes with an individual time trial into milan on stage 21 but it's a touch longer than we've seen over the last few years so just shy of 30 kilometers in length now we don't know exactly who's going to be racing the gyro italia just yet if there are any gc riders who rely on their time trial that is of course the moment to try and do a demulan no not stopping on the side of the road for number two but rather stealing the magliarosa on the very final day of the entire race so there it is a first look at the route for the 2021 jira italia i am incredibly excited because i absolutely love the giro and i also can't wait to enjoy it with more of our audience than ever before don't forget that if you are unable to watch any of those stages live you will be able to catch up either on demand after the race itself or with short or long form highlights and we're going to have daily analysis of everything too we'll also have our usual preview show nearer the time when we have a better idea of which rides will be heading to italy this may however after looking at that route i think it suits a writer like eagan bernal who has said that he wants to do the giro d'italia this year it's never too early to give the g some kiss of death after all who do you think this course suits this year and who do you actually want to see on the start line of the giro make sure you let us know in the comments section just down below and also if you haven't done it already get yourself subscribed to gcm plus before the 28th of february so you can catch all of the action live and on demand ad free for 50 off the normal price thanks very much for watching i'll see you again very soon
